content:
LINUX | SYSADMIN | TECHNOLOGY | SOFTWARE ENGINEERING | PROGRAMMING
Beginners focus on commands. Experienced engineers think in workflows.

32 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Commands change, but the workflow remains the same.
Commands are merely tools. They can vary between environments, distributions, versions, or team preferences. One system might only have top, while another uses htop. Some teams rely on grep, while others prefer rg (ripgrep). That's why memorizing commands isn't enough.
For example, if we need to inspect running processes, the exact command is secondary. The important thing is knowing what information we need to gather and how to interpret it. Once we understand the goal, choosing the appropriate tool becomes straightforward. The workflow stays the same even when the commands change.
In this article, we’ll move from memorizing commands to understanding practical Linux workflows used by engineers to solve real-world problems.
#1. High Memory Usage
High memory usage is one of the most common alerts on Linux systems. The usual first step is to run free -h, which…
